Feature Interaction Decoupling Report

Generated: 2026-03-06 03:16:25

1. Analysis Target

Feature 1SiO2
Feature 2vinyl
Method: SHAP interaction values → 2-D binned averaging (grid 30×30) → Gaussian kernel smoothing (σ = 3.00) → zero-contour boundary extraction → connected-region labeling.
Difference from global SHAP interaction heatmaps: Global SHAP produces raw (un-smoothed) interaction heatmaps for ALL feature pairs as a qualitative overview. This analysis performs targeted decoupling of this specific pair with smoothing to extract robust synergy / antagonism region boundaries.

4. Region Descriptions

  • Synergy: when SiO2 ∈ [10.21, 19.39] and vinyl ∈ [0.0686, 3.067], these two features cooperate to increase the prediction (avg interaction = +0.0001)
  • Antagonism: when SiO2 ∈ [10.21, 39.79] and vinyl ∈ [0.0686, 9.731], these two features counteract each other, reducing the prediction (avg interaction = -0.0001)
  • Synergy: when SiO2 ∈ [19.39, 39.79] and vinyl ∈ [3.734, 9.731], these two features cooperate to increase the prediction (avg interaction = +0.0001)
  • Antagonism: when SiO2 ∈ [35.71, 39.79] and vinyl ∈ [6.066, 9.731], these two features counteract each other, reducing the prediction (avg interaction = -0.0000)
